expect: Designated PHIL claims have structural weak points exploitable from L-1649/L-1653 evidence — at least 1 of 3 can be argued for DROP
actual: FALSIFIED. Neither PHIL-8 nor PHIL-5a meet DROP criteria. PHIL-8 DROP test revealed OPPOSITE: compaction is UNDERCHARACTERIZED, not overcharacterized. L-1580 measured lesson-level (4.4%) but token-level removal is 43.8% of gross growth. PHIL-5a ratio improved 1.48x→1.55x.

phil8_test.description: Attention-only vs compaction growth model comparison (OLS)
phil8_test.verdict: DROP criterion NOT MET — compaction adds significant explanatory power

phil5a_test.verdict: DROP criterion NOT MET — ratio improved from 1.48x to 1.55x

key_finding: L-1580 measurement error: lesson-level compaction (4.4%) ≠ token-level compaction (43.8%). The 10x discrepancy means PHIL-8 Evolve through distillation is UNDERCHARACTERIZED, not overcharacterized. Compaction is not hygiene — it removes 43.8% of total produced tokens.
designated_claim_score: 0/3 DROPPED (PHIL-5a, PHIL-8 tested, PHIL-16 concurrent S545)
confirmation_attractor_note: F-EPIS3 prediction (0/3 DROPPED by S561) continues to hold. Adversarial test produced a genuine finding but in the OPPOSITE direction — evidence strengthens the designated claim rather than weakening it.
external_grounding: Shannon rate-distortion (L-1571): compaction as lossy compression. Lehman 2nd Law (S505 challenge): software systems require increasing investment to maintain order.
